currency_tips: Always carry local currency for street vendors and markets. ATMs widely available. Haggling expected at markets.
tipping: Not expected but appreciated. Round up or 10% at restaurants.
visa_notes: Many countries offer visa-free or visa-on-arrival for most passports

currency_tips: Euro accepted everywhere except Switzerland (CHF). Cards widely accepted. Get a no-foreign-fee credit card.
tipping: Service charge usually included. Small tip (5-10%) appreciated in restaurants.
visa_notes: Schengen visa covers most countries. 90 days visa-free for many nationalities.

currency_tips: Japan is still very cash-heavy. IC cards (Suica/Pasmo) essential for Tokyo transit. China uses WeChat/Alipay almost exclusively.
tipping: Not customary and can be considered rude in Japan. Not expected elsewhere either.
visa_notes: Japan offers visa-free for many nationalities. China requires advance visa with varying rules.

currency_tips: Cards accepted almost everywhere in US/Canada. Mexico uses pesos but USD accepted in tourist areas.
tipping: Expected in US/Canada: 18-22% at restaurants, $1-2 per drink at bars. Mexico: 10-15%.
visa_notes: ESTA required for US visa waiver countries. eTA for Canada. Mexico is visa-free for most.

currency_tips: Carry USD as backup. Argentina has complex exchange rate situation - check blue dollar rate. ATM fees can be high.
tipping: 10% at restaurants is standard. Not expected for taxis.
visa_notes: Most countries visa-free for western passports. Brazil requires e-visa for some nationalities.

currency_tips: UAE dirham and others pegged to USD. Turkey uses lira with high inflation - prices fluctuate. Cards widely accepted in cities.
tipping: 10-15% in restaurants. Baksheesh culture in some countries.
visa_notes: UAE and Turkey offer visa-on-arrival or e-visa for most. Jordan Pass includes visa + Petra entry.

currency_tips: Cashless society, cards accepted everywhere. Download banking apps. Both countries use their own dollars.
tipping: Not expected but rounding up is fine. No tipping culture like the US.
visa_notes: ETA required for Australia. NZeTA for New Zealand. Both have working holiday visa programs.

currency_tips: Haggling is expected and part of the culture. ATMs available in cities. Carry small bills for tips and vendors.
tipping: Small tips expected for most services. 10% at restaurants.
visa_notes: Morocco is visa-free for many. Egypt offers visa-on-arrival or e-visa.

currency_tips: India is increasingly digital (UPI payments). Maldives is expensive - budget for resort markups. Cash still king in Nepal.
tipping: Not mandatory but appreciated. 10% at nice restaurants in India.
visa_notes: India requires e-visa in advance. Nepal offers visa-on-arrival. Sri Lanka has ETA system.

currency_tips: Czech and Hungary use their own currencies (CZK, HUF), not Euro. Croatia adopted Euro in 2023. Cards widely accepted in cities.
tipping: 10% at restaurants is standard. Round up for taxis.
visa_notes: All in Schengen area - same 90-day visa-free rules as Western Europe.
